page: Microsoft Azure EC128ieds_v5 is an isolated, memory-optimized virtual machine instance belonging to the ECIEDV5 server family. It is built on the x86_64 architecture and powered by an Intel processor. The instance features 128 dedicated virtual CPUs managed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, ensuring dedicated compute allocation without resource sharing at the CPU level.

This server type is equipped with 768.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 6.0 GB per vCPU. For local storage requirements, the configuration includes 2891 GB of local disk capacity. Additionally, the instance is designated as Premium Storage capable, allowing integration with high-throughput external storage volumes to support demanding input and output operations.

The hardware profile provides high resource density, combining a large memory footprint with substantial local storage and dedicated compute resources. This configuration is designed for workloads requiring isolation and significant memory capacity, such as large-scale databases, memory-intensive enterprise applications, and data warehousing. It does not include integrated graphics processing units or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.
description: Microsoft Azure EC128ieds_v5 is an isolated, memory-optimized virtual machine instance from the ECIEDV5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 128 dedicated vCPUs built on the x86_64 Intel processor architecture. The hardware configuration delivers 768.0 GB of system memory, which equates to 6.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. For storage, the instance includes 2891 GB of local disk capacity and is Premium Storage capable, supporting high-performance storage configurations. There are no integrated GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses included with this server type. This high-density resource profile is engineered for memory-intensive enterprise applications, large relational databases, and workloads requiring physical hardware isolation.
